summary: Re-ran full backend pytest suite after SMTP connect try/except fix. 28/28 tests passed in 14.45s. The previously critical bug (SMTP connect outside try/except causing HTTP 500) is resolved — both /api/campaigns/send and /api/external/send now correctly return 200 with campaign_id and throttle echo while logging per-recipient failures when SMTP is unreachable.
seed_data_creation: None — used existing fixtures from backend_test.py
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 28 backend tests pass. SMTP-failure path is covered by TestCampaignSend::test_send_campaign_smtp_fails_logs_written and TestThrottle::test_today_sent_unaffected_by_failed_sends, both green. No frontend testing was requested in this iteration.
rca of the issue: Previous issue: SMTPSession.__enter__() raised socket/connect errors outside the per-recipient try/except, propagating an unhandled exception → HTTP 500. Fix verified: connect is wrapped, error captured in smtp_connect_err and re-raised inside each per-recipient try/except, so each recipient row gets a failed send_log and the endpoint returns 200 with campaign_id + throttle echo.
